title: HippoCamp: Benchmarking Contextual Agents on Personal Computers
reason: A realistic personal-computing agent benchmark with device-scale filesystems that exposes major gaps in retrieval, grounding, and multimodal evidence use.

title: Universal YOCO for Efficient Depth Scaling
reason: Combines recursive computation with the YOCO architecture to improve depth scaling and inference efficiency without exploding KV cache costs.

title: $\texttt{YC-Bench}$: Benchmarking AI Agents for Long-Term Planning and Consistent Execution
reason: A sharp benchmark for long-horizon agent planning that surfaces compounding execution failures rather than just one-shot task success.

title: CliffSearch: Structured Agentic Co-Evolution over Theory and Code for Scientific Algorithm Discovery
reason: Builds a more scientifically disciplined agentic discovery loop by evolving theory and code together with originality and correctness gates.

title: ORBIT: Scalable and Verifiable Data Generation for Search Agents on a Tight Budget
reason: Shows a low-cost path to useful search-agent training data with verifiable multi-step queries and open, reproducible generation.

title: Embarrassingly Simple Self-Distillation Improves Code Generation
reason: A surprisingly strong result: simple self-distillation materially improves code generation without verifiers, teacher models, or RL.

title: Screening Is Enough
reason: Replaces relative softmax competition with thresholded absolute relevance and gets strong long-context performance with fewer parameters and lower latency.

title: Online Reasoning Calibration: Test-Time Training Enables Generalizable Conformal LLM Reasoning
reason: Pairs conformal calibration with test-time training to cut reasoning cost while keeping reliability under distribution shift.

title: S0 Tuning: Zero-Overhead Adaptation of Hybrid Recurrent-Attention Models
reason: Finds a clean zero-overhead PEFT surface for hybrid recurrent-attention models by tuning only the initial recurrent state.

title: Detecting Multi-Agent Collusion Through Multi-Agent Interpretability
reason: Pushes interpretability into the multi-agent setting and offers a promising white-box signal for detecting covert coordination.
